user_intent: Forensic evidence photograph showing a side-by-side comparison of two bloodstain patterns on a light tile floor: Drop A is a perfectly round circular blood drop indicating a 90-degree perpendicular impact, and Drop B is a highly elongated elliptical blood drop with satellite spatter trailing from its pointed end indicating a low-angle oblique impact. Labeled with white text. Shot overhead with a macro lens under even clinical forensic lighting.

subject.description: A single perfectly round circular blood drop on a light-colored tile floor. The drop has smooth, well-defined edges with no distortion or spines. Deep crimson center gradually thinning to translucent red at the perimeter. The surface tension creates a slight dome shape catching overhead light with a subtle specular highlight. Approximately 8mm in diameter. Indicates a 90-degree perpendicular impact angle.

A highly elongated elliptical blood drop on a light-colored tile floor. The drop is stretched into a narrow teardrop or oval shape with a length-to-width ratio of roughly 4:1. The leading edge is rounded while the trailing edge tapers to a sharp point with distinct satellite spatter — small secondary droplets scattered in a trail extending beyond the pointed end. The main stain shows directional flow patterns within the blood. Deep crimson with visible surface tension. Indicates a low-angle oblique impact of approximately 10-20 degrees.

scene.location: light-colored ceramic tile floor in a forensic laboratory or crime scene documentation area
background_elements: light beige ceramic tile with subtle grout lines
clean uniform surface with no debris
